We recommended the following and the Management agreed to direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to spearhead the establishment of the GAD Database to gather information such as gender statistics and sex disaggregated data to serve as inputs for planning, budgeting, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended and the Management agreed to encourage the JO and contractual personnel to register as SSS self-employed members in adherence to the provisions of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-83 in order to avail of the benefits and protection against hazards and disability, sickness, maternity, old age, death and other contingencies that the SSS offers.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Treasurer require all Revenue Collectors to prepare and submit their individual monthly RAAF for consolidation in her monthly CRAAF using the prescribed form pursuant to Section 51 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II and submit the same to the Audit Team for verification not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month.

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following to
ensure proper control and presentation of accounts in the Financial
Statements:
For the Municipal Treasurer to:
• Demand liquidation / remittance of all collections made by the
collectors as at year-end with validation of the unused official
receipts vis-à-vis collections declared
• Instruct the personnel in-charge of the preparation of the RCDs to
update the preparation of the RCD especially as at year-end and
submit the same to the accounting office before the closing of the
books
For the Municipal Accountant to:
• Prepare the adjusting entries to correct the net misstatement of the
income accounts of CY 2020 by considering the collections of CY
2020 that were recorded in CY 2021 to present fairly the affected
accounts in the succeeding years.
• Henceforth, instruct the accounting personnel concerned to record
all collections relating to all other taxes, fees, charges and other
revenues in a cash basis of accounting pursuant to GAM Volume I.

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following:
For the Municipal Treasurer:
• Ensure issuance of Official Receipts upon receipt of any collections
even if it is an internal transaction of the Municipality such as fund
transfers between Municipality’s funds.
For the Municipal Accountant
• Ensure that all disbursement vouchers pertaining to fund transfers
between Municipality’s funds are duly supported with official
receipts.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following to present fairly the receivable accounts of the Municipality
in the Financial Statements:
a. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to
ensure that these are collected when these become due and
demandable and that cash advances are liquidated within the
prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose
b. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all
outstanding cash advances from the concerned officials/employees
who are still connected with the Municipality
c. Impose sanctions prescribed in item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No.
1997-0021 for non-compliance of liquidating cash advances, such as
withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the
officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to
comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges
against them
d. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer
connected with the Municipality but whose whereabouts are still
known to liquidate their cash advances/ receivables and consider
taking legal actions/remedy against them.
e. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation/payment of
the named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are
already deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is
not known (e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and
request for the write off of the receivable for fair presentation of the
account in the Financial Statements taking into consideration the
liability of those individuals who granted the clearances. Exclude
for request for write-off individuals whose receivable arise from
disallowances and charges, cash shortages and subject of a pending
case in court or before investigative authorities.
f. Make representation with the ICFAU to fast tract the issuance of
the credit notice, if warranted, to close the long outstanding
advances to special disbursing officer account
g. Seek legal assistance to ensure collection of the receivable from the
absconding Municipal Treasurer

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following to further improve reliability and accurateness of financial
reporting:
a. Adjust the remaining payable accounts if proven to be
undocumented and without existing claimants especially those
accounts which are outstanding for more than 2 years as stated in
Section 98 of PD 1445
b. Review the validity of accounts payable in a regular basis to
promptly determine which of the accounts are already dormant and
to take necessary remedial measures
c. Investigate the negative balances to make the necessary demand on
the receivable of the management against the concerned
supplier/creditor/employee, if applicable
d. Ensure to recalculate and review all payments made to payees
before approving the voucher to minimize overpayments.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to
present the current portion of long-term loans as of year-end as Loans
Payable- Current in the financial statements in compliance with Section
367 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs Volume I, for
purposes of fair and proper presentation of the accounts in the financial
statements.

Therefore, in view of above observation/gaps noted, we recommended and
the management agreed to do the following;
For the Municipal General Service Officer to;
a. Develop, formulate, and implement strategies, policies, and
procedures in the conduct of complete Physical Inventory of PPEs
and reconciliation of balances with the Municipal Accounting
Office;
b. Provide complete information on the inventory report such as
property number and value, location and status of the property on
all the PPEs reported for easy identification. Use the prescribed
format of the Inventory Report specified in the Government
Accounting Manual for LGUs; and
c. Conduct disposal of the unserviceable properties and submit the
necessary documents to the Office of the Municipal Accountant as
basis of the latter in dropping the PPEs in the books of accounts.
For the Municipal Accountant to;
a. Coordinate with the GSO and the inventory team, after the conduct
of complete inventory count, for the reconciliation of the PPE with
the books and the inventory report and make the necessary
adjustments in the books.
b. Reconstruct the PPEs with noted “for reclassification” so that
proper amounts will be presented in the Financial Statements; and
c. Coordinate with the Municipal Engineer on the remaining CIP
accounts and transfer all completed PPAs to proper PPE accounts
in consideration with the certificate of completion, inspection report
and other necessary documents from the Municipal Engineering
Office;
d. Draw journal entry vouchers, if proper disposal has been made, to
derecognize the disposed properties and apply appropriate
accounting procedures to effect adjustments in the books.
For the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Augment manpower needs of the General Service Officer, if budget
permits, to ensure immediate conduct of the physical count.

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to do
the following;
a. Make necessary effort to provide information of the PPEs not
provided with depreciation by coordinating with other
Offices/Departments and provide the depreciation if complete data
has been gathered;
b. Check the accuracy of information entered in the accounting system
and make sure all data of the PPEs are provided;
c. Recompute the computation of depreciation once entered into the
system to verify and establish the correct amount;
d. Adjust the understatement of Accumulated Depreciation in
previous years to be able to present the PPE at its proper valuation.
e. Coordinate with the system provider of the accounting system used
by the Accounting Office to correct the computation of the
provision for depreciation

Therefore, we recommended and management agreed to do the following;
For the Municipal Accountant to;
a. Review the PPEs to be reclassified to proper Inventory Account
with estimated useful life of more than one year but small enough
to be considered and recorded as PPE and the semi expendable
items that do not meet the 15,000 thresholds;
b. Record the items as expense upon issuance of small tangible and
semi expendable items; and
c. Reconcile records with the GSO so that proper monitoring, control
and accountability can be assured.
For the General Services Officer to:
a. Issue corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip to the personnel to
whom the small tangible and semi expendable items are issued;
and
b. Conduct annual separate inventory-taking and report on
inventoriable supplies and materials which considers semi-
expandable items costing 15,000 and below and small tangible items
with estimated useful life of life of 1 year per Annex A of COA
Circular No. 2005-002 dated April 14, 2005.

Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive, through the
Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, BAC Chairman and other
personnel concerned agreed to do the following:
• Discontinue/stop the practice of procuring goods through cash
advances.
• Instead of Cash Advances, consider GPPB 06-200 dated March 25,
20208 on advanced payments to supplier for mobilization adhering
to needed supporting documents as embodied in the Circular
• Conduct the procurement activities in accordance with the
provisions in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and guidelines
provided by GPPB resolutions, specifically during state of calamity
• Ensure that required documents are attached to the liquidation
reports of cash advances before certifying that the cash advances
are liquidated for proper evaluation of the transactions.
• Make the supplier accomplish the Omnibus Sworn Statement
(OSS) and attach the same to the Disbursement Voucher for
procurements undertaken under Bayanihan to Heal as One Act

Therefore, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant, Municipal
Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer agreed to control and stop the
release of cash advances to elected officials in the ensuing year for purposes
other than the official travelling expense of the said official.

Therefore, we recommended and management, through the Sangguniang
Bayan, agreed to comply with the existing provisions as regards the
realignment expenditures to ensure that legislation enacted is sufficient
and appropriate in accordance with the provision of law.

Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
direct the Local School Board of the Municipality to;
a. Formulate a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization
policies and to coordinate with the concerned National Government
Agencies and the Provincial School Board, to carry out the planning
and budgeting activities for the SEF;
b. Prepare the SEF budget using the SEF Budget Preparation Form
No. 1 as stated in the JC;
c. Incorporate to the SEF budget the ECCD programs and provide
specific activities and projects following the purpose of the funding
as stated in Section 4 of the JC; and
d. Conduct consultation/discussion/hearing with the schools
concerned and other stakeholders. Thereafter, consolidate all
budget requests from all schools in the LGU to have a sound
scheduled activities that will be funded out of the SEF in
consideration with the duly funded programs of the DepEd
National Office

Therefore, we recommended and the management agreed to do the
following to enhance utilization of the 20% Development Fund:
For the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to:
• Formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively
implement the projects and prioritize what should be accomplished
for its timely use without sacrificing the quality of work;
• Look into the unimplemented development projects under the 20%
Development Fund and determine causes of non-implementation.
Formulate a timetable that will determine the exact period when the
project will be implemented and monitor the status of its
implementation by calling the attention of the concerned offices.
• Provide complete data on the monitoring report as to percentage
status, period of implementation and total cost incurred. Reconcile
with the Accounting and Engineering Office so that proper amount
and status of the project will be presented in the report.
For the Municipal Engineer to:
• Consider implementing some of the projects “by contract” to the
qualified contractors so that numerous PPAs will be implemented
as scheduled.
• Coordinate with the MPDO and Accounting Office with regards to
the total cost incurred of the project so that correct data will reflect
on the submitted report.

Therefore, we recommended and the MDRRMO agreed to do the
following:
• Closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects funded out of
LDRRMF to avoid/minimize disaster projected and unexpected
risks, and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities
at all levels.
• Conduct review on a quarterly basis on the status of
implementation of PPAs, and communicate with the implementing
office on their assigned PPAs that were not yet utilized for timely
implementation.
• Recommend for the realignment of PPAs that are seen to be not
viable for implementation to optimally use the DRRM Funds to
circumvent disaster risks

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of PPE and come up with the government required form of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2019 and every year thereafter and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts.
Require the issuance of Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for all procured or acquired properties which shall be used to report on the receipt of property and equipment for official use from the Designated Property Officer.

We recommended the following:
Comply strictly with the rules and regulations as provided in Section 89 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;
Monitor strictly the liquidation of unliquidated cash advances; and
Before signing/approving the clearances of employees, management official should diligently check their respective records of accountability to avoid incurrence of unliquidated cash advance.

We recommended that the Accountant exhaust all means for the reconciliation of the accounts which have negative balances and prepare the appropriate adjustments, if necessary.  Should it be established that there is overpayment, cause the eventual refund from the individual involved and effect the proper adjustment in the books of accounts.

We recommended the following:
Submit, at once, the duly reconciled BRS for January to December 2019 and henceforth, regularly submit the monthly bank reconciliation of the Municipality; and
The Municipal Accountant comply with Section 10 of NGAS Manual, Volume II on the reconciliation of SL balances with the controlling accounts; and pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the preparation of BRS to achieve an accurate financial reporting of Cash in Bank account.

We recommended that the management:
Strictly observed the schedule of implementation in accordance with the Annual Investment Plan;
Mobilize the concerned offices in the early planning and implementation of development projects to avoid incurring possible additional cost as a result of delayed implementation; and
Submit justifications for the non-implementation and delayed completion of the above-mentioned priority projects totaling P13.584 million.

We recommended for the submission of the aforementioned reports within the reglementary period to avoid the risk that decision-makers and other users could not make relevant decisions due to the failure of the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly, quarterly and year-end trial balance and other financial reports on time.  Continued violation of the law will warrant suspension of payment of the salary until she shall have completed the requirement of the Commission.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer strictly comply with the regulations on the submission of the DVs and their supporting documents and RCDs and their official receipts to the COA Audit Team to avoid delays in the verification and conduct of other audit-related activities.
We recommended that Municipal Accounting and Municipal Treasurer’s Offices remit all the taxes withheld intact within the prescribed period to avoid penalties/surcharges.

We recommended the following:
Observe the perpetual inventory method of accounting for inventories pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual, Volume I;
Require all Department Heads/requisitioning units to prepare the RIS for every request for supplies issuances for submission to the Supply Officer; and
Require the Supply Officer to prepare the monthly MSSMI duly supported by RIS from end-users for submission to the Accounting Office for proper recording of issuances in inventories.

We recommended that the management must take necessary action on the settlement of the Notices of Suspensions, Notices of Disallowance and Notices of Charge as of December 31, 2019 in the total amount of P11.323 million.

1.9. Therefore, we recommended for the Municipal Accountant to do the
following:
 Prepare bank reconciliation report on a monthly basis for every
depository account of the Municipality to accurately present the
balances of the account in the Financial Statements.
 Confirm with the AGDB if those unconfirmed accounts, were already
closed. If such is the case, retrieve the documents from the Municipal
Treasurer or any concerned holder and make the necessary adjustments
 Request for a bank snapshot from the AGDB to fast track the
preparation of the bank reconciliation

2.9. Therefore, we recommended and management agreed on the following
measures to be carried by the Municipal Accountant to present fairly the
Cash in Bank-LCCA account in the Financial Statements:
a. Locate and trace down the documents to support the adjusting entries
on the long outstanding reconciling items
b. Coordinate, reconcile and investigate the book errors;
c. Record adjustments for valid reconciling items per books as reflected
in the BRS prepared;
d. Coordinate with the bank personnel relative to the identified bank
adjustments/errors and make a representation to make the necessary
adjustments;
e. Locate the stale checks if it is still with the Municipal Treasurer and
make the necessary adjustments. If the check is not available, confirm
the payable account with the payee and make the proper procedures
for replacing the check. If no response is received, make the necessary
adjustments with the books.

3.8. Therefore, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive will do the
following:
a. Instruct the Local Finance Committee to employ trending analysis of
data to facilitate realization of the reasonable targeted budget and
ensure proper management of financial affairs of the Municipality.
b. For the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to
regularly monitor and evaluate the status of cash availability in relation
to restricted cash balances such as continuing appropriation,
obligations to be settled and unobligated allotments to prevent further
occurrence of cash deficits
c. For the Municipal Treasurer to analyze collection
shortfalls/deficiencies to identify and implement revenue enhancing
strategies to further improve collection efficiency
d. For the accountable officers to practice restraints and prudence in the
disbursement of funds

4.9. Therefore, we recommended and management agreed that the Municipal
Treasurer will do the following:
 Maintain individual cashbook for each special account of the
Municipality
 Refrain from disbursing funds without cash back up
 Inform concerned chief of office handling the accounts on the
insufficiency of balances on required disbursements so that proper
subsidy of funds will be recorded and accounted
 Ensure that disbursements are charged against the appropriate bank
accounts

5.7. Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that
in future endeavors, when there are surcharges/penalties, these must be
paid out of the personal funds of the person causing the surcharges and not
chargeable against government funds otherwise it will be disallowed in
audit to conform with COA Circular no. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

5.8. Moreover, it was advised that the Municipal treasurer will refrain from
incurring disbursements when the cash in bank balance is already at its
required maintaining balance.

6.5. Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to deposit
collections received from the collectors with the authorized depository
bank daily or not later than the next banking day

6.6. We further recommend for the Municipal Mayor to prioritize the
provision for available government vehicle to be utilized by the Municipal
Treasurer in depositing collections on time.

7.10. Therefore, we recommended that Municipal Accountant will do the
following to present fairly the receivable accounts of the Municipality in
the Financial Statements:
a. Conduct regular monitoring and analysis of receivable accounts to
ensure that these are collected when these become due and demandable
and that cash advances are liquidated within the prescribed period
depending upon their nature and purpose
b. Exert effort to demand immediately the liquidation of all outstanding
cash advances from the concerned officials/employees who are still
connected with the Municipality
c. Impose sanctions prescribed in item 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 1997-
0022 for non-compliance of liquidating cash advances, such as
withholding the salaries and other emoluments of the
officials/employees who continuously disregard, fail or decline to
comply with the demand, including filing appropriate charges against
them
d. Send demand letters to officials and employees who are no longer
connected with the Municipality but whose whereabouts are still
known to liquidate their cash advances/ receivables and consider taking
legal actions/remedy against them.
e. Gather all documents that will prove that liquidation/payment of the
named individuals is no longer possible such as those who are already
deceased (e.g. death certificate) and whose last address is not known
(e.g. retirement certification, clearances, etc.) and request for the write
off of the receivable for fair presentation of the account in the Financial
Statements taking into consideration the liability of those individuals
who granted the clearances. Exclude for request for write-off
individuals whose receivable arise from disallowances and charges,
cash shortages and subject of a pending case in court or before
investigative authorities.
f. Make representation with the ICFAU to fast tract the issuance of the
credit notice, if warranted, to close the long outstanding advances to
special disbursing officer account
g. Seek legal assistance to ensure collection of the receivable from the
absconding Municipal Treasurer

8.11. Therefore, we recommended and management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant will do the following to further improve reliability and
accurateness of financial reporting:
a. Confirm the amount of the payables from the list of the identified
creditors to determine which of the payables are still valid and bonafide
obligation and make necessary adjustments in the books
b. Adjust the payable accounts if proven to be undocumented and without
existing claimants especially to those accounts which are outstanding
for more than 2 years as stated in Section 98 of PD 1445
c. Review the validity of accounts payable in a regular basis to promptly
determine which of the accounts are already dormant and to take
necessary remedial measures
d. Review and investigate the negative balances and make the necessary
demand on the receivable of the management against the concerned
supplier/creditor/employee, if applicable; and
e. Make the necessary adjustments in the books should review and
investigation of the negative balances were due to erroneous recording.
f. Investigate, reconcile and make the necessary adjustment for the
deferred real property tax and special education tax accounts

9.5. Therefore, in view of the above observations/gaps noted, we recommended
and management agreed to do the following:
For the General Services Officer – Designate to:
 Immediately conduct the physical count of the Municipality’s
properties and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and IInventory and Inspection Report
of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP)
 Provide property numbers on physically counted assets for identity of
the properties;
 Update the Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE) on all
properties counted, which were in the hands of accountable officers.
 Submit to Municipal Accountant the RPCPPE and IIRUP for
reconciliation purposes. Investigate discrepancies noted for proper
adjustments in the books.
For the Municipal Mayor to:
 Augment manpower needs of the General Services Office, if budget
permits, to ensure immediate conduct of the physical count
For the Municipal Accountant to:
 Make the necessary adjustments in the books, if necessary, upon
reconciliation of the accounting records and the RPCPPE and IIRUP
submitted by the GSO
 Follow-up with the service provider to make the proper adjustments
into the accounting program to produce correct depreciation schedule
 Update the depreciation expense of Flood Control System and
Communication Equipment accounts
 Refer to the Certificate of Completion in transferring completed
Construction in Progress projects to its proper PPE account.
Thereafter, provide depreciation on transferred PPAs
 Exert effort to reconstruct the list of the PPE accounts which are for
reconciliation coordinating closely with other departments especially
CGSO for proper substantiation of accounts
For the Municipal Engineer to:
 Regularly submit to the Municipal Accountant all certificate of
completion of PPAs for proper updating of accountant’s record.

10.9. Therefore, to have a more reliable financial reporting and effective
property management of the agency over the assets. We recommended and
management agreed to do the following:
a. For the Property Custodian to:
 Strictly observe the utilization of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS)
upon the issuance of small tangible items for proper monitoring and
accountability
 Utilize ICS for semi-expendable items costing P10,000.00 and below
for monitoring and accountability purposes
 Conduct annual separate inventory-taking and report for all
furniture, fixtures, equipment, books and work animals costing
P10,000.00 and below (semi-expendable items) and small tangible
items
b. For the Municipal Accountant to:
 Review the PPE list to identify items needed to be excluded from the
PPE accounts relative to the small tangible items and semi-
expendable items
 Make proper adjustments in the books on identified capitalized
small tangible items and semi-expendable items
 Coordinate with the GSO for the updating of inventory reports and
reconciliation.

11.10. Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Planning and
Development Office ensure the inclusion of all the on-going as well as all
projects that are to be implemented and are yet to be implemented for the
reporting year in consideration with the actual amounts incurred to
warrant transparency and provide auditable information that could be
verified by all stakeholders of the LGU for sound decision-making.

11.11. We further recommended to reconcile the amounts incurred by the MPDC
with the records of the Accounting Office.

12.10. Therefore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Budget
Officer, Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Engineer, Municipal
Accountant, Municipal Planning & Development Officer and those
concerned in the implementation of projects to stop the charging of
administrative expenses against the development fund and conform to the
DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-01. Henceforth, it is advised that the
Municipality will strictly adhere to the provisions of the JMC especially on
PPAs that are not allowed to be charged against the 20% development fund
otherwise these disbursements will be subjected to disallowances.

13.11. Therefore, we recommended and management agreed that the MDRRMO,
in coordination with the MDRRMC, will do the following:
a. Allocate enough funds from the General Fund (GF) to subsidize the
maintenance and other operating expenses (MOOE) of the CDRRM
Office, in order not to lessen the fund intended for disaster related
activities.
b. Revisit the CDRRM Plan and make the necessary adjustments to
ensure alignment of PPAs with JMC No. 2014-1 of the NDRRMC,
DILG, DBM and CSC

14.15. Therefore, we recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to
enjoin the following Municipal Officials to do the following:
Municipal Planning and Development Officer
a. Closely monitor the utilization and implementation of priority projects
for the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and
environmental management outcomes and to maximize the usage of the
fund;
b. In coordination with implementing office, ensure that program of
works, project designs, coordination with other agencies and other
needed documents needing approval had been prepared and approved
without objections prior to its inclusion to the Annual Investment Plan;
c. Recommend to the Municipal Development Council to reprogram
savings out of the completed projects (both from continuing and
current appropriation)
d. Evaluate projects and ensure specificity of PPAs before presentation to
the Municipal Development Council for inclusion to AIP to minimize
unearmarked projects.
e. Evaluate continuing appropriations if PPAs are still viable to be
implemented and recommend result of evaluation to the Municipal
Development Council for proper discharge accruing to developmental
needs of the Municipality
f. Strict compliance with the Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of
the Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of
Budget and Management dated April 13, 2011 should be observed.
Municipal Engineer
a. Fast track the implementation based on the scheduled time frames
indicated in the AIP by the implementing department concerned to
achieve the desired management outcomes
Implementing Department:
 Coordinate with the Municipal Engineering office and ensure that
program of works, project designs, coordination with other agencies
and other needed documents needing approval had been prepared and
approved without objections prior to its inclusion to the Annual
Investment Plan
Municipal Mayor
a. Augment, manpower needs, preferably an engineering graduate, of
the Municipal Engineering Office and Municipal Planning and
Development Office.

15.14. Therefore, we recommended the following:
For MDRRMO to:
 Closely monitor the undertaking of proposed projects funded out of
LDRRMF to avoid/minimize disaster projected and unexpected risks,
and enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities at all
levels.
 Utilize the unexpended portion of the Trust Fund of DRRMF and
prepare a comprehensive CDRRMF Plan to include both the current
appropriations and unexpended balance in the Special Trust Fund.
 Conduct thorough situational analysis (research and development) of
the Municipality to understand risk information which includes
national hazards, vulnerabilities and climate change risks for
development to alleviate poverty and environmental protection
consistent with the purpose of the fund to identify immediately needed
activities to prioritize PPAs and to guide in the specificity of PPAs.
For Municipal Budget Officer to:
 Monitor balances of appropriation and ensure that expenditures will
not exceed provided appropriation
 Request immediately approval of supplemental budget insufficient
amount of appropriation

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor issue an Executive Order creating the Monitoring and Evaluation Team to perform such functions as enumerated under Section 5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer implement and closely monitor the implementation of all the municipal projects to make sure that they are implemented as budgeted or planned in a given year.  He should likewise submit justification/reasons for its non-implementation.

observations.recommendation:
In the annual planning for the MEEDO, management must adopt a realistic estimate of income that has far more reasonable assurance of being realized, as this will be the basis for the budget, cap the spending ceiling, and help achieve economic viability.

In implementing the MEEDO functions, projects and activities during the year, quarterly income must be monitored, with corresponding adjustment made in the levels of spending.

Creating new positions in the MEEDO should be held in abeyance.

Most important of all, re-assess the viability of the MEEDO in the medium and long term and at the same time rationalize its operations and personnel.

observations.recommendation:
1. The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board be reconstituted to conform to the membership requirement under RA 9003 and its IRR in order to optimize the involvement of civil society as envisioned by law.

2. The Board regularly convene to enable effective performance of its functions and refrain from relegating its duties and responsibilities to a single person.

The MSWM Board faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, specifically the following:

1. Prepare IEC campaign materials for distribution.  They should be crafted in accord to the level of understanding of the stakeholders in order to saturate the target stakeholders in its entirety.

2. Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAPP).


3. Collaborate IEC campaigns with the Barangay officials to achieve proper coordination and synchronization.  Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns. 

4. Follow-ups on IECs be conducted regularly in the whole municipality to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.

The management should, through its Sangguniang Bayan, enact Municipal Ordinance to enforce the collection of garbage fees in the entire municipality.  Similarly, a Municipal Ordinances for the imposition of fines and penalties to those violators relative to the implementation of the Solid Waste Management Act shall be enforced accordingly.
